retry_reason: put_net_track always decrements the refcount and never fails; retrying would cause a double-put (use-after-free).
contract_summary: This is a put wrapper that releases a reference on a network namespace that was previously acquired (typically in the corresponding bpf_iter_init_seq_net function). It is called when the BPF iterator is done using the namespace, and after this call the caller must not access the net pointer. The operation is unconditional and cannot fail. The wrapper does not return any value; it simply decrements the reference count with put_net_track, which may free the namespace if the count reaches zero.
